center.name: 路德維希·賴興巴赫
center.summary: 路德維希·賴興巴赫是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联10种鸟类，包括斑头雁、斑长脚鹬、南极鹱等10种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Pilherodius pileatus Río Amacayacu, Amazonas, Colombia The capped heron (Pilherodius pileatus) is a water bird endemic to the neotropics, inhabiting rainforest from the center of Panama to the south of Brazil.Ridgely, R

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The tufted tit-spinetail (Leptasthenura platensis) is a species of bird in the Furnariinae subfamily of the ovenbird family Furnariidae. It is found in Argentina, Brazil, Uruguay, and possibly Paraguay.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: | parent_authority = Reichenbach, 1853 | taxon = Thalassoica antarctica | authority = (Gmelin, JF, 1789) | range_map = Thalassoica antarctica map.svg | range_map_caption = }} The Antarctic petrel (Thalassoica antarctica) is a boldly marked dark brown and white petrel, found in Antarctica, most commonly in the Ross and Weddell Seas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The sharp-billed treehunter (Heliobletus contaminatus), sometimes called the sharp-billed xenops, is a species of bird in the Furnariinae subfamily of the ovenbird family Furnariidae. It is found in Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Bar-headed goose The bar-headed goose (Anser indicus) is a goose that breeds in Central Asia in colonies of thousands near mountain lakes and winters in South Asia, as far south as peninsular India

birds.evidence: Gray]], 1840 | species = leucocephalus | authority = (Vieillot, 1816) |synonyms = Recurvirostra leucocephala Vieillot Recurvirostra orientalis Cuvier, 1816 Leptorhynchus pectoralis Du Bus, 1835 Himantopus palmatus Gould Cladorhynchus australis Lawson Cladorhynchus leucocephalus rottnesti Mathews Xiphidiorhynchus pectoralis Ludwig Reichenbach, 1845 |synonyms_ref = | range_map = Bandedstiltrgemap.png | range_map_caption = Banded stilt natural range }} The banded stilt (Cladorhynchus leucocephalus) is a nomadic wader of the stilt and avocet family, Recurvirostridae, native to Australia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The red-tailed comet (Sappho sparganurus) is a medium-sized hummingbird belonging to tribe Lesbiini of subfamily Lesbiinae, the "coquettes". It is found in Argentina and Bolivia and possibly Chile and Peru

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The tawny-breasted parrotfinch (Erythrura hyperythra) is a species of estrildid finch found in Indonesia, Malaysia and the Philippines

birds.evidence: |year=2022 |title=Chloebia gouldiae |volume=2022 |article-number=e.T22719744A211561819 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2022-1.RLTS.T22719744A211561819.en |access-date=17 August 2024}} | status2 = EN | status2_system = EPBC | status2_ref = | genus = Chloebia | parent_authority = Reichenbach, 1862 | species = gouldiae | authority = (Gould, 1844) | synonyms_ref = | synonyms = | range_map = Gouldian Finch.png | range_map_caption = Distribution }} The Gouldian finch (Chloebia gouldiae), also known as the Gould's finch or the rainbow finch, is a colourful passerine bird that is native to Australia
